frame.length_semantics: payload byte at offset 2 counts command byte plus argument bytes

checksum.algorithm: xor over all bytes from DLE STX through DLE ETX inclusive, then append 1 checksum byte
checksum.note: Same framing/checksum model as the existing DSP408 proxy decoder; all frames in the first FIR408 log were valid.

frame.frame_detection: start at DLE STX, end at DLE ETX; frame detection does not rely on a separate outer frame length

layout_hypothesis.rule: Input GEQ read storage is linear per input and per band. Each band uses one u16le. Bands advance by +2 bytes across usable block offsets 5..53, then wrap to the next block at offset 5. Input changes follow a +3 blocks / -10 bytes start-offset stride.

tooling_notes.note: The Java DSPD toolchain now supports full FIR408 config reads through block 0x1F via readBlocks(), readConfigBlocks(), readSaveConfig(), and ReadBlockSet. Older scripts may still use direct write("00 01 02 27 XX", 0x24) requests.

Scripts that inject read/probe payloads should establish a fresh proxy-side session with connect(); resetSession(); ensureSession(); handshake(); before sending active reads. Attaching to a stale GUI-ended session can log PC_TO_DSP frames without receiving DSP_TO_PC responses.
